mode_note: mix.lock is UNCHANGED vs git HEAD (git diff HEAD -- mix.lock is empty). Zero (old,new) pairs resolved, so the 8 tarball-diff rules had no changed package to scan and did NOT run. All findings below come from Step 4 (mix hex.audit) plus Step 5 OSV/Hex API enrichment. This is an advisory audit of the CURRENT locked tree, not a change audit.

external_tools.mix_audit: not installed - skipped, not installed per Iron Law 2
external_tools.osv_scanner: not installed - skipped, not installed per Iron Law 2

packages.remediation: bump to 1.9.3 (clears all three; finch constraint is ~> 1.8 so no conflict)

findings.message: Unbounded HTTP/1 response-header and chunked-trailer accumulation causes memory-exhaustion DoS
findings.applicability: client-side; qx is an HTTP client via lib/qx/hardware/ibm.ex

findings.message: Zero-length HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frames bypass Mint's header-block byte-size cap and exhaust client memory
Sign-tolerant HTTP/1 chunk-size parser enables response smuggling against strict intermediaries on pooled connections
HTTP Response Splitting via Non-VCHAR Bytes in cow_http_struct_hd:escape_string/2
findings.downgrade_reason: not in prod dependency tree; never reaches published qx_sim

findings.message: Cookie Request Header Injection via Unvalidated Encoder in cow_cookie:cookie/1
multipart :length limit is not charged for part headers, enabling unbounded temp-file creation (DoS)

verdict_reason: mint 1.9.1 carries 3 CVEs (1 HIGH) and IS in the shipped prod tree via req -> finch -> mint, so published qx_sim consumers inherit them. scripts/audit_shipped.sh exits 1.
release_gate: Do not cut a qx release on the current lock. Bump mint to 1.9.3 first.
mutations_made: none - skill is non-mutating; mix.exs and mix.lock untouched
